description_long: This object is a vintage-style computer monitor with a boxy, rectangular form and a slightly weathered, off-white exterior. The front features a large, glass-covered CRT display with green text on a dark background, surrounded by a thick, black bezel. To the right of the screen are two vertical slots labeled '1' and '2', each with a corresponding black lever and orange or white icon, suggesting manual controls or input selectors. The sides and back are plain, with no visible vents, ports, or additional features, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The surface appears to be made of painted metal or plastic, with subtle scuff marks and a matte finish. The monitor's overall appearance is reminiscent of industrial or early personal computing equipment, suitable for use in urban environments such as control rooms, garages, or public kiosks.
description: A boxy, vintage computer monitor with a thick black bezel, CRT display, and two vertical control levers on the front right. The exterior is off-white and slightly worn, with a utilitarian, industrial look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the CRT screen with green text, thick black bezel, and two vertical control levers labeled '1' and '2' on the right.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, off-white surface with no visible features or controls.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a flat, featureless off-white panel with no visible ports or ventilation.
description_view_3: Right side view with a plain off-white surface and a black strip along the rear edge, likely part of the casing.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, black, and rectangular, with sharp corners and a matte finish, providing a strong visual contrast to the off-white body. It frames the display area with no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: No visible stand; the monitor appears to rest directly on its flat base, which is integrated into the main body and made of the same material.
attribute_button_placement: Two vertical levers or sliders are located on the front right side, arranged vertically and labeled '1' and '2'. No other buttons are visible.
